subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the concept of habitat and identify its biotic and abiotic components
Differentiate between population and community within ecosystems
Analyze interactions between organisms and their environment
Interpret food chains, food webs, and trophic levels
Understand the ecological roles of producers, consumers, and decomposers
Evaluate the impact of environmental changes on ecosystems
Recognize the importance of ecosystem conservation and sustainable practices

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Habitats
topics_covered: Examples of terrestrial and aquatic habitats
teaching_strategy: Use images and diagrams of different habitats
Ask students to share examples of habitats around them
student_activities: Identify two habitats near school or home
List living and non-living components in each habitat

lesson_plan.title: Biotic and Abiotic Components
teaching_strategy: Discussion about plant dependence on sunlight and water
student_activities: Create a comparison table of biotic and abiotic components
Observe a school garden and classify components

lesson_plan.title: Population and Community
teaching_strategy: Use diagrams showing groups of organisms
student_activities: Count the number of specific plants or insects

lesson_plan.title: Ecosystems and Interactions
teaching_strategy: Use flow diagrams to show interactions
student_activities: Explain interactions among at least three organisms

lesson_plan.title: Food Chains and Trophic Levels
student_activities: Construct a food chain from given organisms
Identify trophic levels of each organism

lesson_plan.title: Food Webs and Decomposers
student_activities: Create a simple food web diagram
Observe decomposing organic matter like leaves

lesson_plan.title: Human Impact and Ecosystem Conservation
topics_covered: Examples such as deforestation and pollution

teaching_aids: Charts of ecosystems and food chains
Pictures of forest, pond, and farm ecosystems
teacher_tip: Encourage students to observe ecosystems in their surroundings such as gardens, ponds, or farms to understand ecological interactions in real life.
